# RealFlow

> The fastest, most complete open-source library for building node-based UIs
> with React. Headless core (`@realflow/core`, zero deps), React renderer
> (`@realflow/react`), and a React Flow compatibility adapter (`@realflow/compat`).
> Culling on by default, built-in undo/redo + auto-layout, and a first-class
> AI-agent operation layer.

This file helps LLMs generate correct RealFlow code on the first try.

## Install

```
npm install @realflow/react   # includes @realflow/core
```

## Minimal app

```tsx
import { RealFlow, Background, Controls, MiniMap } from '@realflow/react';
import '@realflow/react/styles.css';

const nodes = [
  { id: 'a', position: { x: 0, y: 0 }, data: { label: 'Hello' } },
  { id: 'b', position: { x: 240, y: 80 }, data: { label: 'World' } },
];
const edges = [{ id: 'e1', source: 'a', target: 'b', animated: true }];

export default () => (
  <div style={{ width: '100vw', height: '100vh' }}>
    <RealFlow defaultNodes={nodes} defaultEdges={edges}>
      <Background />
      <Controls />
      <MiniMap />
    </RealFlow>
  </div>
);
```

Rules:
- The container MUST have an explicit size.
- Prefer `defaultNodes`/`defaultEdges` (uncontrolled) + `useRealFlow()` over
  React Flow-style `onNodesChange` boilerplate.
- Node `data` is arbitrary; `data.label` is shown by the default node.

## Imperative API (useRealFlow)

```tsx
const flow = useRealFlow(); // inside <RealFlow> or <RealFlowProvider>
flow.addNode({ id, position: { x, y }, data: { label } });
flow.connect({ source, target });
flow.updateNodeData(id, { label });
flow.removeNodes([id]);
flow.layout('layered');   // 'layered' | 'tree' | 'force' | 'radial' | 'grid'
flow.fitView({ duration: 300 });
flow.undo(); flow.redo();
```

## Custom nodes

```tsx
import { Handle, type NodeProps } from '@realflow/react';
function MyNode({ data }: NodeProps<{ label: string }>) {
  return (
    <div>
      <Handle kind="target" side="left" />
      {data.label}
      <Handle kind="source" side="right" />
    </div>
  );
}
// <RealFlow nodeTypes={{ myType: MyNode }} ... />  and node.type = 'myType'
```
Handle props: `kind: 'source'|'target'`, `side: 'left'|'right'|'top'|'bottom'`,
optional `id`, `dataType` (typed ports), `maxConnections`.

## AI agent operations (@realflow/core)

An LLM should emit an array of operations; apply them with `applyOperations`:

```ts
import { applyOperations, operationSchema, OPERATIONS_PROMPT,
         describeGraph, toMermaid } from '@realflow/core';

applyOperations(store, [
  { op: 'add_node', id: 'fetch', label: 'Fetch data' },   // omit position => auto-layout
  { op: 'add_node', id: 'clean', label: 'Clean' },
  { op: 'connect', source: 'fetch', target: 'clean' },
  { op: 'set_status', id: 'fetch', status: 'running', message: 'batch 3/10' },
]);
```
- Ops: add_node, update_node, remove_node, connect, remove_edge, update_edge,
  set_status, select, layout, fit_view, focus_node, clear.
- Never throws; invalid ops are returned in `result.errors` for self-correction.
- One `applyOperations` call = one undo entry.
- Use `operationSchema` as an LLM tool `input_schema` (Anthropic) /
  `parameters` (OpenAI). Use `describeGraph(store)` or `toMermaid(store)` to
  give the model the current graph cheaply.

## Migrating from React Flow

Change imports to `@realflow/compat` (and CSS to `@realflow/compat/style.css`).
`ReactFlow`, `Handle` (`type`/`position`), `Position`, `useReactFlow`,
`useNodesState`, `applyNodeChanges`, `addEdge` all work. See docs/migration.md.
